title: AWS Complete Learning Quiz
description: Test your AWS knowledge across IAM, EC2, VPC, S3, Lambda, and more.

questions.question: In AWS IAM, what takes the highest priority when evaluating permissions?
options: A. Identity-based policies
B. Resource-based policies
C. An explicit Deny statement in any policy
D. AWS managed policies

questions.question: What is the difference between IAM roles and IAM users?
options: A. Roles are for humans; users are for services
B. Roles provide temporary credentials assumed by AWS services/users; users have permanent credentials
C. Users can have multiple roles simultaneously; roles can only have one user
D. Roles are global; users are region-specific

questions.question: An EC2 instance in a private subnet needs to download OS updates. What AWS resource enables this?
options: A. Internet Gateway
B. Virtual Private Gateway
C. NAT Gateway (in a public subnet)
D. VPC Endpoint

questions.question: What does S3 'versioning' protect against?
options: A. Unauthorized access to bucket objects
B. Accidental deletion or overwriting of objects — keeps all versions
C. Cross-region data transfer costs
D. Object size limits

questions.question: Which EC2 pricing model offers the greatest discount (up to 90%) but can be interrupted?
options: A. Reserved Instances
B. Dedicated Hosts
C. Spot Instances
D. Savings Plans

questions.question: What is the difference between RDS Multi-AZ and Read Replicas?
options: A. Multi-AZ is for disaster recovery (standby replica, synchronous, no reads); Read Replicas are for read scaling (asynchronous)
B. Multi-AZ allows read traffic; Read Replicas do not
C. Multi-AZ uses asynchronous replication; Read Replicas use synchronous
D. They are the same feature with different names

questions.question: What is a VPC endpoint used for?
options: A. Connecting VPCs to the internet
B. Allowing VPC resources to access AWS services privately without routing traffic through the internet
C. Establishing VPN connections to on-premises networks
D. Exposing internal services to the internet

questions.question: In AWS Lambda, what causes a 'cold start'?
options: A. Running a Lambda function for the first time ever
B. Lambda spinning up a new execution environment when no warm instance is available
C. A Lambda function that takes more than 30 seconds to execute
D. Exceeding the concurrent execution limit

questions.question: What is the maximum retention period for S3 Glacier Deep Archive?
options: A. 90 days
B. 1 year
C. 7 years
D. No maximum — you set the retention period

questions.question: Which DynamoDB feature allows reading data at its most recent state, even in a multi-writer scenario?
options: A. Eventually consistent reads (default)
B. Strongly consistent reads
C. Transactional reads
D. DynamoDB Streams

questions.question: What is AWS GuardDuty?
options: A. A firewall for EC2 instances
B. A threat detection service that continuously monitors AWS accounts using ML and threat intelligence
C. A web application firewall (WAF)
D. An identity governance service

questions.question: In S3, what is the purpose of a presigned URL?
options: A. Permanently makes an S3 object public
B. Grants temporary, time-limited access to a private S3 object without requiring AWS credentials
C. Signs an S3 bucket policy cryptographically
D. Creates a signed version of the S3 bucket name

questions.question: What is the difference between an ALB and NLB?
options: A. ALB operates at Layer 7 (HTTP) with path/host routing; NLB at Layer 4 (TCP/UDP) with ultra-low latency
B. ALB is for internal traffic; NLB is for internet-facing
C. ALB supports WebSocket; NLB does not
D. ALB can have static IPs; NLB cannot

questions.question: What is AWS KMS envelope encryption?
options: A. Encrypting data with multiple CMKs simultaneously
B. Using a CMK to encrypt a data key, then using the data key to encrypt actual data — CMK never leaves KMS
C. Wrapping encryption keys in an envelope for transit
D. A method to encrypt KMS keys with other KMS keys

questions.question: What is the purpose of an IAM permissions boundary?
options: A. Sets the maximum permissions an IAM entity can have — even if identity policies grant more, boundary limits them
B. Creates a firewall around IAM users
C. Defines minimum permissions required for an AWS account
D. Restricts which regions an IAM user can access

questions.question: Which AWS service would you use to automatically rotate database credentials?
options: A. AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store
B. AWS Secrets Manager
C. AWS KMS
D. AWS IAM

questions.question: What is DynamoDB's RCU consumption for 1 strongly consistent read of a 7KB item?
options: A. 1 RCU
B. 2 RCU
C. 3.5 RCU
D. 7 RCU

questions.question: What is the purpose of an SCP (Service Control Policy) in AWS Organizations?
options: A. Encrypts data across all accounts in the organization
B. Sets maximum permission guardrails for member accounts — even if identity policies allow, SCP can deny
C. Manages service quotas across the organization
D. Controls which AWS services are available per region

questions.question: Which Route 53 routing policy routes based on the health of your endpoints with an active-passive failover?
options: A. Latency routing
B. Weighted routing
C. Failover routing
D. Geolocation routing

questions.question: What is IRSA (IAM Roles for Service Accounts) in EKS?
options: A. An IAM role that only allows read access to S3
B. A mechanism that lets Kubernetes pods assume IAM roles via service account annotations, avoiding node-level permissions
C. Identity-based Resource Scheduling Algorithm
D. An AWS service for managing Kubernetes RBAC

questions.question: What is the purpose of CloudFront origin access control (OAC)?
options: A. Controls which origins CloudFront can access on the internet
B. Ensures only CloudFront can access S3 objects — blocks direct S3 URL access
C. Provides access control for CloudFront admin console
D. Controls edge location selection for caching

questions.question: In AWS, what is the Shared Responsibility Model for EC2 instances?
options: A. AWS manages everything including the OS
B. AWS manages hypervisor and physical infrastructure; customer manages guest OS, patches, apps, and security groups
C. Customer manages hardware; AWS manages software
D. Both AWS and customer share equal responsibility for all layers

questions.question: What does Aurora's 'cluster endpoint' route traffic to?
options: A. All Aurora instances randomly
B. Only read replicas
C. The primary (writer) instance
D. The instance with lowest latency from the client

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of using Spot Instances in an Auto Scaling group?
options: A. Guaranteed availability with no interruptions
B. Up to 90% cost reduction for fault-tolerant workloads that can handle interruptions
C. Faster instance startup compared to On-Demand
D. Free egress data transfer

questions.question: What AWS service provides a centralized view of security findings from GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, and others?
options: A. AWS CloudTrail
B. AWS Config
C. AWS Security Hub
D. Amazon Detective
